title: Hard-code 2025 Monaco and wet-exemption legality states
summary: Official FIA rules changed Monaco for 2025 to a dry-race two-stop / three-set requirement, while use of intermediates or wets changes the usual dry-compound obligation.
rationale: Official FIA rules changed Monaco for 2025 to a dry-race two-stop / three-set requirement, while use of intermediates or wets changes the usual dry-compound obligation. That creates rule-driven pit calls that generic tyre-life heuristics can miss.
implementation_hint: Build a per-row rule state from `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, `PitStop`, and prior stint history: dry-stop debt, distinct dry compounds used, sets used, wet-exempt flag, and remaining mandatory stops under `Monaco 2025` versus all other races. Interact with `LapsRemaining_Est` and current compound.
expected_effect: Should reduce false negatives on regulation-driven stops, especially in 2025 Monaco and mixed-condition races.
risk: Niche coverage; gains may be small in CV unless folds expose the relevant race-year edge cases.
